title: Calonemorchis demissa
intro: Calonemorchis demissa, also known as the forgotten calanthe, is a type of orchid native to tropical regions of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects. The bioactive constituents of Calonemorchis demissa include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Calonemorchis demissa is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used to make infusions and topical creams to utilize its medicinal benefits.
